mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales (RSAFS adjusted for CPIAUCSL inflation) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, which drives equity valuations higher over a 20-day forward window. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring a specific trigger threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:S&P 500 baseline positive skew of 65.5% suggests demand-driven rallies dominate
failure_condition: Rank IC between real sales growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off behavior and negative 20-day forward returns as investors anticipate earnings pressure and recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market weakness historically precedes equity drawdowns
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, or Rank IC between unemployment change and forward returns exceeds -0.02 in magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate and inflation (CPIAUCSL) jointly determine real monetary conditions. When FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL inflation by more than 2 percentage points, real rates are restrictive, compressing equity multiples and producing negative 5-day forward returns as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates at 2025-09-01 snapshot show FEDFUNDS 4.22% vs inflation 323.364 index level
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated real rates
failure_condition: Rank IC of real rate spread with 5-day forward returns remains below 0.01 in magnitude, or SPX returns are positive in > 60% of high real-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates corporate earnings capacity and profit margins. A sustained month-over-month increase in INDPRO of > 0.2% signals supply-side strength that drives positive 60-day forward returns as the market reprices earnings growth upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 2025-08-01 shows revised value 101.6247, indicating recent production activity
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03, or positive INDPRO signals precede negative returns in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly capture demand-side momentum in residential investment and labor income. When both rise simultaneously (HOUST > 1,300k annualized and PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1%), the economy is in a demand-driven expansion that produces positive 20-day forward returns as equity investors gain confidence in sustained growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2025-08-01 revised to 1291.0k, near threshold; PAYEMS at 158472k
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of joint HOUST-PAYEMS signal with 20-day returns falls below 0.04, or positive signal instances produce negative returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 2-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive 60-day forward returns as the market reprices growth and valuation multiples expand.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2025-09-01 is 4.22%, providing room for easing
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or post-shock policy response
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS decline and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03, or easing periods produce negative returns in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth is a continuous always-on signal of labor market health. Sustained month-over-month PAYEMS growth drives positive 5-day forward returns as strong employment supports consumer spending and corporate earnings without requiring a specific trigger threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 2025-08-01 revised to 158472k, indicating sustained employment base
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02, or correlation becomes negative in expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and real sales (RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL) jointly measure aggregate demand strength. When both are accelerating (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5% annualized and RSAFS real growth > 0.3% monthly), the economy enters a demand-driven expansion that produces positive 60-day forward returns as equity investors gain confidence in sustained earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2.5% annualized and RSAFS real growth > 0.3% monthly
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC of joint GDPC1-RSAFS signal with 60-day returns falls below 0.05, or positive signal instances produce negative returns in > 50% of cases, or the mechanism fails to distinguish expansion from contraction regimes.
